what_it_does: Runs three structural-coherence detectors from runtime/coherence_detect.py: a hard GATE (registry-vs-live) that asserts every nodes/*.py node-type file matches the live registry, and two REPORT-only candidate detectors (capability-with-no-consumer and hardcoding-candidates) that print findings but only assert they return lists.
observations: The only failing assertions come from registry_vs_live; the other two detectors are explicitly non-blocking.
A global PASS counter is incremented only when check() assertions succeed.
It bootstraps a NodeRegistry, Suite, and a temporary FsStore to obtain live capabilities.
A large docstring upfront classifies detectors by trust tier and warns that AST-based reports have false positives by construction.
Candidate detector output is printed every run for human or stronger-model adjudication.
Paths nodes/, runtime/, and ROOT are hardcoded relative to the test file location.
standouts: Explicit two-tier policy: GATE vs REPORT and trustworthy vs candidate.
Positive-only discipline: candidate detectors are never allowed to fail the suite.
The gate is described as a pure set difference with no model or heuristic.
conventions: Uses sys.path.insert to make package imports available.
Uses tempfile.mkdtemp for an isolated store.
Docstring narrates design rationale before code.
Global mutable counter for tallying passed checks.
concerns: Global PASS counter and check() side effect make state implicit.
Candidate detectors only check isinstance(..., list), so they do not verify detector logic or output quality.
Relies on coherence_detect functions whose implementation is not shown in this file.
Hardcoded directory names (nodes, runtime) may break if project layout changes.
notes: A two-tier acceptance harness: one hard registry-consistency gate plus two intentionally non-blocking candidate detectors that report for human review.
questions: What exact heuristic defines a node-type file in registry_vs_live?
What AST scope is used to decide no consumer for Suite methods?
What thresholds classify a big UPPER-name literal in hardcoding_candidates?
How are false positives from dynamic dispatch filtered, if at all?
Is this intended to run in CI with only the gate enforced?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the docstring says it surfaces a hard gate and two candidate-only reports, and the code does exactly that.
intention_signals: To institutionalize a policy where only trustworthy, model-free comparisons can fail the build, while heuristic AST detectors must propose candidates for review rather than enforce.
novelty: The explicit GATE vs REPORT and candidate-only, positive-only, never a gate framing embedded directly in an acceptance test.
contribution: A runnable boundary object that enforces which coherence checks are build-blocking and which are advisory, preserving the distinction for downstream fusion or CI policy.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test for structural coherence detectors that asserts a hard registry-vs-live gate and runs two non-blocking candidate detectors for unused capabilities and hardcoded literals.
intention_for_embedding: To validate that pure registry-to-disk consistency is enforced while AST-based heuristic detectors remain advisory reports for human adjudication.

symbols.does: Test helper that asserts a condition and increments a global pass counter.
symbols.description: check(label, cond) takes a human-readable label and a boolean condition, asserts the condition with a FAIL message, and increments the module-level PASS counter on success. It is used to record both gate assertions and the lightweight existence checks for candidate detectors.
